highlights: Performing vulnerability assessments on web applications and internal infrastructure.
Performing penetration testing tasks and delivered findings with actionable remediation guidance.
Reporting the found vulnerabilities into document with valid proof of concepts which helps the team.
Developing tools using python to know and understand their working principle.
Conducted end-to-end VAPT and reconnaissance on web apps and network infrastructure, successfully uncovering high-severity flaws (SQLi, XSS, IDOR) using tools like Burp Suite and Nmap.
Assisted senior team members in validating exploits and authored detailed, CVSS-scored penetration testing reports with proof-of-concept evidence.
Coordinated 'LOOP: Last One On Peak,' an attack and persistence-based hacking competition simulating real-world adversary tactics.
Provided technical infrastructure assistance and support for various cybersecurity events, including Capture The Flag (CTF) challenges and specialized hardware lab workshops.
Completed intensive, lab-based training covering ethical hacking methodologies, network penetration testing, and web application security under the OCSP curriculum.
Gained hands-on proficiency with offensive security tools including Metasploit, Burp Suite, Nmap, Wireshark, Gobuster, and WPScan across controlled lab environments.
Studied Active Directory attack paths including enumeration, Kerberoasting, Pass-the-Hash, and privilege escalation techniques within Windows environments.
Earned the Offenso Certified Security Professional (OCSP) certification upon successful completion of practical examination.
